commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; this MEMBER-authored public trust/API PR needs maintainer acceptance and normal merge gating.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect found; the trust signal remains server-derived from exact officialPublishers rows and the PR-head spec documents that boundary. (sha=51a25fe73bdfa1747c873998923a3f95d7c5ecfb)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a server-derived optional `owner.official` field to public skill/package/search data and renders Official owner badges across skill lists, skill detail, and plugin detail UI.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a PR review rather than an issue reproduction. I inspected current-main source, the PR diff, and the contributor's before/after screenshots for the visible behavior.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; this MEMBER-authored public trust/API PR needs maintainer acceptance and normal merge gating.

target.title: fix: show official owner badges across public surfaces
target.body:
## Summary - Adds one server-derived owner trust signal: `owner.official`. - `owner.official` is true only when the exact owner publisher has an `officialPublishers` row. - Threads that field through public skill lists/search, skill detail, package detail, v1 HTTP API responses, schemas, docs, and badge rendering. - Uses the field to show the compact Official badge consistently across browse rows/cards, skill detail owner metadata, and plugin owner metadata. ## What Changed | Area | Change | | --- | --- | | Official policy | Keeps the current row-backed policy. No org membership inference and no uploaded metadata can mark a publisher official. | | Convex public skill data | Public skill detail and digest-backed list/search entries can include `owner.official`. | | Public package data | Package detail owner payloads can include `owner.official`, including plugin detail data. | | v1 HTTP API | `/api/v1/skills/search`, `/api/v1/skills/:slug`, and `/api/v1/packages/:name` can return optional `owner.official`. | | Schemas/docs | Public schema packages and docs accept/document optional `owner.official`. | | UI | Badge calculation reads `owner.official`, so Official badges survive route changes, sorting, search, and package/plugin fallback paths. | | Tests | Adds focused coverage for official publisher policy, public skill/package/search data, v1 HTTP serialization, schema parsing, badge logic, list rows/cards, and package detail UI. | ## Intentional Surfaces Several public pages are built from different backend shapes: live skill rows, search digests, package detail, and HTTP API fallback data. This PR makes those shapes agree on the same `owner.official` field. | Surface | Why this is in scope | | --- | --- | | Skill detail: `/$owner/$slug` | A skill owned by an official publisher should show the same owner badge as publisher pages. | | Browse/search: `/skills`, `/skills?sort=...`, filters, and search results | Sorting/filtering/searching should not change whether the Official badge appears. | | Browse cards and rows | The backend can now send `owner.official`; visible cards/rows need to use it. | | Related, trending, highlighted, and audit skill entries | These use shared public entry shapes and should not drift from browse/search behavior. | | Package detail API: `/api/v1/packages/:name` | Plugin pages load through this API, so owner badges need the same field there. | | Skill search/detail API: `/api/v1/skills/search` and `/api/v1/skills/:slug` | API clients and fallback UI should keep the official owner signal when using skill endpoints. | | Plugin detail UI: `/plugins/$name` | The title-level Official tag already existed; this adds the missing owner-level badge. | ## Before / After Evidence Screenshots were captured locally from before/after runs and are hosted on `pr-evidence/2407-official-badges` so this PR can show the images without adding screenshot files to this diff. | Area | Before | After | | --- | --- | --- | | Main browse: `/skills?sort=downloads` | The official publisher skill had no compact Official badge.
| The same skill now has the compact Official badge from `owner.official`.
| | Official publisher skill detail | No compact Official owner badge and no title Official tag.
| Owner badge appears and the title Official tag appears through `owner.official`.
| | OpenClaw plugin detail: `/plugins/@openclaw/whatsapp` | Title Official tag existed, but owner metadata missed the compact badge.
| Title Official tag remains and owner metadata shows the compact Official badge.
| ## Post-Merge Follow-Up After this lands, investigate `openclaw install` behavior: - Flag malicious or suspicious packages/skills during install. - Add the intended Official bypass for that install-time handling. ## Validation - `bun run format:check` passed. - `bun run lint` passed. - `bun run test convex/lib/officialPublishers.test.ts convex/skills.public.test.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts convex/skills.publicListCursor.test.ts convex/skills.publicModeration.test.ts convex/skills.staffAuditLogs.test.ts convex/skills.versions.public.test.ts convex/search.test.ts packages/schema/src/schemas.test.ts src/lib/badges.test.ts src/components/SkillListItem.test.tsx src/routes/skills/-SkillsResults.test.tsx src/__tests__/package-detail-route.test.tsx` passed: 14 files, 611 tests. - `bun run test convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts packages/schema/src/schemas.test.ts` passed: 2 files, 294 tests. - `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit` passed. - `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit` passed.
target.author: jesse-merhi

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
